name: FUN_00139b40
reason: Faithful lift kept dormant by score gate. VC71 86.3% (73 candidate vs 66 reference insns) measured against fresh per-function ref delinked/functions/00139b40.obj. Gate B passes (condition holds): callees {0x99530 real_a_rgb_color_to_pixel32, 0x180770, 0x1ba140 tag_get, 0x180b10} and sole caller 0x18ca40 are all outside the GC/lifecycle cluster; function only appends a record to the per-frame light-marker array at 0x5a8f6c (count 0x5a90ac, max 8, 0x28 stride) -- no object garbage/lifecycle mutation. Score gap (~7 insns) is the float-inequality guard: original emits per-component memory-operand FCOMP (FLD [color+N]; FCOMP [0x2533c0]) while VC71 on the C || chain hoists *(float*)0x2533c0 to a register and emits register fucompp; plus tail integer-codegen scheduling (orl $-1 vs movl, count/+0x20 store order). A correctly-pinned permuter run (delinked/functions/00139b40.obj, 300s/4 threads, 2026-06-22) found lower-score candidates; none confirmed to cross the 88% activation bar this pass (an earlier 367-iter run was vacuous -- it mis-selected stale ref 00085110.obj). Re-evaluate in a dedicated permuter-apply session before activation. Equivalence is the wrong lane: two callees are XCALL literal-address casts -> --allow-stubs makes the side-effect store diff stub-vs-stub (vacuous, per feedback_equivalence_hardcoded_import). param_6 confirmed float (caller MOV+PUSH no FILD; callee 0x180770 FLD [EBP+8]); kb decl updated int->float param_6.

name: object_choose_random_change_colors
reason: Faithful @<eax>-defined lift kept dormant. VC71 67.0% (190 candidate vs 168 reference insns) via objcopy-bridged compare_obj against fresh per-function ref delinked/functions/0013e1f0.obj (vc71_verify auto-path cannot bridge the real-name<->FUN_ symbol map on this 12k-line TU; symbol _object_choose_random_change_colors is present in the compiled candidate, confirmed by objdump). Gate B passes: callees {FUN_0007c270 color-blend cdecl, x87_fmod} are pure math with no GC/lifecycle edge; writes only the object change-color slots and their +0x30 mirror. Equivalence is the only activation lane but is vacuous on zero-fill (permutation count comes from tag data = 0, so the CDF loop body never runs); needs a live object-appearance state snapshot not currently captured. Disasm-derived (FABS-on-input not output, push-then-fstp arg5 recovered). Safe dormant.

name: vector_to_line_distance_squared3d
reason: Faithful cdecl lift, dormant pending a fix for a REAL residual divergence. CORRECTION 2026-06-22: the earlier 'equiv unverifiable via harness' was WRONG. The harness IS usable -- with a constants snapshot (static .rdata float consts 0.0/0.5/1.0/parallel-eps/len-eps/REAL_MAX seeded byte-exact from cachebeta.xbe; recipe regenerates artifacts/snapshots/real_math_consts.json) AND invoking unicorn_diff BY NAME not address (the candidate .obj symbol is the decl name, not FUN_<addr>), the universal-NaN ('0 data refs') is GONE and equiv runs 15/60 pass with real finite divergences (67.9% cov, high conf; INSN 245 oracle vs 273 lifted). So ce10 has a REAL lift bug, NOT a harness artifact. VERIFIED CORRECT: n = p2 x p4 (cross), non-parallel s,t = (delta x d2).n/|n|^2 and (delta x d1).n/|n|^2 (standard line-line closest-point), FUN_0010cd40 is plain cdecl(point,seg_start,seg_dir) and call arg-order is right. Bug is ISOLATED to the non-parallel s_oob/t_oob clamp classification (or the parallel projection): seed-56 oracle 0.00097 vs lifted 6.5e18 = an in-range-vs-out-of-range misclassification picking a far clamp endpoint. Activation pending that clamp-path fix; harness now ready to debug it. UPDATE 2026-06-30 (instruction-level disasm verification, advances the above): the 'misclassification' is NOT a clamp-logic or call-arg bug -- ruled out at instruction level. Disassembled 0x10ce10 fully (0x10ce10-0x10d378): entry holds EDX=p1, EBX=p3, ESI=p2, EDI=p4 in registers throughout. s_oob call (0x10cfda) = FUN_0010cd40(closest_a, EBX=p3, EDI=p4); t_oob call (0x10d02d) pushes ECX=&closest_b, EDX, ESI=p2 so arg2=EDX. Disassembled FUN_0010cd40 (0x10cd40-0x10ce01): it clobbers only EAX/ECX+FPU, NEVER touches EDX/EBX/ESI/EDI -- so EDX==p1 still holds at the t_oob call even when s_oob ran first. The C body's flagged assumption (line ~2027 'FUN_0010cd40 preserves EDX which still holds p1') is CONFIRMED CORRECT; both call arg-orders match disasm exactly; s/t formulas + s_oob/t_oob classification match disasm exactly. Therefore the large divergences (seed-56 0.00097 vs 6.5e18) are FP-ORDER BRANCH-FLIPS: clang -mno-sse computes s/t with a different x87 operation order than MSVC's exact FPU sequence (oracle: FDIVR inv first, then nx*inv per term @0x10ce95+; C: (cross)*nx*inv per term), producing sub-ULP s/t differences that straddle the [0,1] clamp boundary and flip in-range<->out-of-range, picking a different endpoint -> amplified output divergence. This is the same x87-rounding structural cap noted for siblings 0x10a5e0/0x10a710. NEXT (lift-score-improve op-order lever, uncertain payoff): reorder the C s/t computation to mirror the oracle FPU sequence and re-run equiv; if pass rate does not materially rise, accept as structural FP cap and keep dormant. Do NOT change clamp logic or call args -- they are byte-faithful.
